title: Hoth (Jedi Lord)
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}}
{{Update|[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]}}
{{Conflicting|''[[Star Wars: Jedi vs. Sith]]''|''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]''}}
{{Youmay|the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who fought in the [[New Sith Wars]]|[[Hoth (disambiguation)|other uses]] of the word [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]|linebreak=1}}

|masters=

{{Quote|I dreamt that I would single-handedly defeat the dark side, that I would make a difference, change the galaxy… the galaxy, however, has changed ''me''.|Hoth|<ref name="Jedi vs Sith 3">[[Jedi vs. Sith 3|''Jedi vs. Sith'' 3]]</ref>}}
[[Lord/Legends|Lord]] '''Hoth''', born '''Rohlan''', was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] and a famous [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] and [[Jedi Lord|Lord]] who led the [[Army of Light/Legends|Army of Light]] against [[Sith Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Skere Kaan]]'s [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]] during the [[New Sith Wars]] until their final confrontation in the [[Ruusan campaign]]. Having joined the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] with a dream of single-handedly ending the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] threat, Hoth trained several students and rose to become the [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple's]] [[Battlemaster/Legends|battlemaster]] and the [[Supreme Commander (Republic)|Supreme Commander]] of the [[Republic Military/Legends|Republic Military]], building his [[Army/Legends|army]] from whatever resources were available, including the recruitment of [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Youngling/Legends|children]]. Hoth fought Kaan and his forces across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], ultimately assaulting the Sith Lord's stronghold on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]].

Aided by his friend [[Pernicar]] and fellow Jedi Lord [[Valenthyne Farfalla]], Hoth prosecuted the campaign against Kaan, facing challenges of low supplies, dwindling morale, and harsh conditions. Hoth's temper was as legendary as his battle prowess, and, after the [[Death/Legends|death]] of Pernicar, a feud between Hoth and Farfalla almost delivered victory to Kaan. Despite their animosity, the pair reconciled, allowing Hoth to face his foe in a final confrontation. Knowing that the desperate Sith would utilize a dangerous [[Force power/Legends|Force power]] known as the [[thought bomb]] in an attempt to stave off defeat, Hoth went to face him anyway, forcing the Brotherhood of Darkness to destroy themselves in the process. Hoth's act of self-sacrifice began the advent of a rebirth of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and ended the Sith as a galactic threat for almost a thousand [[Standard year/Legends|years]].

==Biography==
===Early life===
{{Quote|You couldn't ask for a better man to lead the Republic war effort.|Republic commander|<ref name="pod">''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]''</ref>}}
[[File:Younglordhoth.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Rohlan imagines destroying the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] as a young man.]]
Born on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kaal/Legends|Kaal]] during the [[Republic Dark Age]], the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] Rohlan was born into a respected lineage of [[Jedi Lord]]s who stood sentinel over the [[Yushan sector/Legends|Yushan sector]].<ref name="EGTW" /> In his youth, Rohlan believed he had the power to end the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] threat to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] single-handedly and make [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] a better place.<ref name="Jedi vs Sith 3" /> To fulfill this dream he followed in his family's footsteps and joined the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], constructing a [[Color/Legends|blue]]-bladed [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. During his teenage years, Rohlan became known as the Knight of Hoth, freeing the [[Corellian Trade Spine/Legends|Corellian Trade Spine]] from Sith [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] during a showdown on the ice world [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]. Once the Republic moved to ensure that the Trade Spine would not fall, Hoth traveled to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and received the title of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] and was nominated to be the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple's]] [[Battlemaster/Legends|battlemaster]]. While Hoth was greatly respected by his students,<ref name="EGTW" /> which included [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|Initiates]] such as [[Dree Vandap]],<ref name="Darkness Shared" /> [[Tal (Jedi)|Tal]],<ref name="jk" /> and [[Johun Othone]], whom he selected as his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]],<ref name="potj" /> stories of Hoth's successes against the Sith were told in whispers around the Temple and kept quiet by the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]].<ref name="EGTW" />

When the High Council attempted to raise Jedi Master [[Skere Kaan]] to prominence as the scourge of the Sith, the Order was dealt a serious blow when their protégé defected and founded the [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]]. Realizing the failure of the Jedi High Council, Hoth left the Temple with a contingent of loyal [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] and rallied the banners of the [[Jedi Grand Council]], mustering the support of all reigning Jedi Lords and massive forces from allies from the [[Javin Marches]], [[Cularin/Legends|Cularin]], [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]], and [[Kamparas]]. Together, Hoth's massive force attacked Kaan's Brotherhood under the banner of the [[Army of Light/Legends|Army of Light]], a Jedi-led militia of Republic forces and loyal vassals. Strapped for resources and soldiers, Hoth gathered the ships and weapons of those Lords loyal to him, overseeing the conversion of [[Freighter/Legends|freighters]] into [[Warship/Legends|warships]] and the recruitment of any Force-sensitive Republic citizens, including [[Youngling/Legends|children]], to augment the ranks of the rapidly dying Jedi and avoid their recruitment into the Sith Brotherhood.<ref name="EGTW" /><ref name="Jedi vs Sith 1" /><ref name="jk" />

===The Army of Light===
[[File:Rohlan_of_Kaal_by_John_Van_Fleet.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Rohlan of Kaal battles Sith pirates on Hoth.]]
Though made in desperation and ill-equipped with nothing but the resources available to the participating Jedi Lords, the Army of Light under Lord Hoth saw resounding success, albeit at a heavy cost. Along with other Republic champions like [[Kiel Charny]] and [[Valenthyne Farfalla]], Hoth drove the Brotherhood from [[Cloak of the Sith/Legends|the Cloak of the Sith]] and countless other [[Planet/Legends|worlds]], including the [[Hoth/Legends|planet which was his namesake]], [[Dromund Kaas/Legends|Dromund Kaas]], and [[Malrev IV]].<ref name="The Dark Forces Saga">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050407a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 1}}</ref> This initial period of victory, however, eventually gave out. The Army of Light was outnumbered by the Brotherhood, and the Sith mounted their own offensive, taking key Republic planets and driving into the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]].<ref name="pod" />

Hoth's response was to unify the Republic's dispersed forces, augmenting the Army of Light. Serving as a [[Jedi General/Legends|General]] for the Republic, Hoth won over half a dozen victories, became a [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]] star,<ref name="pod" /> and, by [[1003 BBY]], had been made [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of the [[Republic Military/Legends|Republic Military]].<ref name="EGTW" /> As they were still outnumbered, he shifted his strategy away from fighting massive armies and [[Navy/Legends|navies]] towards targeting the Sith themselves, particularly their leaders, the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lords]]. To this end he focused many of his Jedi on an [[Third Battle of Ruusan|assault]] on the Sith-held world of [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]], hoping to draw Kaan's armies away from the Core. Kaan chose to take his Sith instead, intending to eradicate the Jedi in one blow.<ref name="pod" />

===The Ruusan campaign===
{{Quote|I'm dying with every Knight… every death is a burning nail in my chest!|Lord Hoth|<ref name="Jedi vs Sith 1" />}}
The first three battles had been fought in [[Realspace/Legends|space]] and had been quick and decisive. But the increased importance of Ruusan to both sides turned the conflict into a bloody stalemate of a ground war that ravaged the surface of the world. Hoth would spend the last two years of his life fighting for the planet.<ref name="pod" />

As the strength of both sides dwindled, Hoth looked for ways to augment his diminishing Jedi forces. Not all Jedi had answered the call to come to Ruusan, believing Hoth had made the war into a personal vendetta and was only prolonging it, or simply choosing to support the Republic on other worlds, and now he sought their aid. Valenthyne Farfalla, whom Hoth had always disliked, volunteered to seek out those Jedi and ask for their help, believing that their refusal was in part due to Hoth's bluntness and that they would respond to his form of diplomacy and persuasion better. He left Ruusan, vowing to return with three hundred [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]]. To preserve what strength he had until Farfalla returned, Hoth changed tactics from open combat to guerrilla warfare, using the planet's rainy season and its vast expanses of forest as cover.<ref name="pod" />

[[File:HothFarfalla.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Hoth and [[Valenthyne Farfalla]] at odds with each other after the death of Pernicar]]
Hoth had fought the Sith doggedly, but the [[Death/Legends|death]] of his close friend [[Pernicar]] in a Sith attack shook his resolute manner for a time. The arrival of Farfalla shortly after the attack, too late to save his friend, drove a further wedge between the two Jedi. Sith tactics, too, had grown more ruthless with the arrival of the aggressive Sith Lord [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]]. The Dark Lords sent a decoy force to attack the Army of Light and then, using the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]], summoned a massive wave of destruction that burnt the forests of Ruusan, slaughtering many. Only the timely arrival of [[Farfalla's Knights|Farfalla's reinforcements]] breaking through the Sith [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] saved them.<ref name="Jedi vs Sith 4">[[Jedi vs. Sith 4|''Jedi vs. Sith'' 4]]</ref>

Beaten down by the never-ending stalemate, the miserable conditions, dwindling troop morale, and the deaths of so many, Hoth began to seriously consider abandoning Ruusan to the Sith, though he knew that this would be a betrayal of all he stood for. The Jedi Master faced an impossible choice. During this crisis of faith, Pernicar appeared to him in a dream. Hoth asked for the advice of his old friend, as Pernicar had often given him while alive, and Pernicar told him to trust in [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], to not give in to despair, and to mend the situation with Farfalla. After awaking, Hoth sent an apology to Farfalla through an [[Unidentified envoy|envoy]], and the two combined their forces to drive back the Sith. The Jedi at last had the advantage, and with Kaan fleeing into the hills and caves and the Army of Light's morale on the rise, Hoth decided to press forward and launch an assault on Kaan's base camp.<ref name="pod" />

===The Seventh Battle of Ruusan===
{{Quote|To drive the darkness away, it seems, the light will have to perish with it!|Lord Hoth|<ref name="Jedi vs Sith 1" />}}
Though he was prepared to do battle, Hoth wanted Kaan to surrender to spare them all from further bloodshed on both sides. Preparation for the final battle was not entirely hopeful. Hoth and Farfalla suspected a trap on the part of the Sith, and the Jedi Tal had a premonition of disaster. Still, the [[General/Legends|general]] saw no other choice and opted to press forward.<ref name="jk" />

Hoth and Farfalla began their attack in the morning. Farfalla led the way, while Hoth brought up the rear. With the Dark Lords summoned by Kaan to the caves to prepare a [[thought bomb]], a devastating burst of Force energy that would eradicate life within its range, the battle was over by the time Hoth's forces arrived, with most of the Sith taken prisoner alive and almost no casualties to the Army of Light. Hoth made his way towards Kaan's encampment but was stopped by Farfalla, who warned him of the thought bomb. Hoth, however, had already [[Force sense/Legends|sensed]] it and knew that he had to stop Kaan from using it to cause even more destruction by either forcing him to detonate it on Ruusan or convincing him to surrender.<ref name="Jedi vs Sith 6" />

[[File:HothKaan.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Jedi Lord|Lord]] Hoth and [[Sith Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Skere Kaan|Kaan]] confront each other.]]
He and the Army entered the canyon where the mouth of the caves lay, past poles carrying the bodies and severed heads of the Jedi who had died in the previous battles. Leaving Farfalla and the bulk of the army outside the caverns and ordering them to get as far from the blast site as possible, he took one hundred Jedi, all volunteers, with him. Together they headed down the tunnels to Kaan's chamber, where he and the Dark Lords waited, the power of the thought bomb filling the air around them. Kaan greeted Hoth, and Hoth responded that the Sith Lord could not frighten him with the prospect of death. Seeing the madness that had come upon the man, Hoth then knew that Kaan would not surrender, and made a lunge at him, attempting to kill him before he could set off the thought bomb. He was too late, however, and with a clap of his hands, Kaan released the power of the dark side of the Force. Lord Hoth and all the Jedi with him were killed.<ref name="Jedi vs Sith 6" /><ref name="pod" /> Their [[Force ghost/Legends|spirits]] remained trapped within the caverns by the power of the thought bomb and would not be freed until Jedi Knight [[Kyle Katarn]] [[Mission to Ruusan|came to Ruusan]] over [[5 ABY/Legends|1,000 years later]].<ref name="jk" />

===Legacy===
{{Quote|Come… history awaits.|Lord Hoth|<ref name="jk" />}}
After Hoth's death, his former Padawan, Johun Othone, petitioned the Jedi and Republic to construct a memorial to the Army of Light on Ruusan. The Jedi turned down his request, wanting to let the tragedy be forgotten, but the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Senate]] chose to honor it. Othone had a mausoleum constructed in what had become known as the [[Valley of the Jedi]].<ref name="potj" /> A statue of Hoth was carved in the Valley, towering above those of the many other members of the Army. When Kyle Katarn visited this site one year after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], he received a vision from the spirit of Tal, in which he saw Lord Hoth from Tal's perspective on the day of the last battle.<ref name="jk" /> Hoth was also honored with the central [[Star/Legends|star]] of the [[Ruusan system/Legends|Ruusan system]] receiving the name [[Hoth's Brand]].<ref name="Kyle Katarn's Tale">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=swminis/article/kylekatarnstale|series=Kyle Katarn's Tale|text=Mission 1}}</ref>

==Personality and traits==
[[File:Hoth-NEGTC.png|thumb|left|200px|Lord Hoth]]
As a young man, Hoth was a dreamer who saw only resounding victory in his mind. He believed that he could single-handedly end the threat that the Sith posed to the galaxy, and he accordingly joined the Jedi Order and rose to the rank of Master.<ref name="Jedi vs Sith 3" /> After winning several victories for the Republic during the New Sith Wars,<ref name="pod" /> Hoth became hardened against the galaxy. By the time of the [[Seventh Battle of Ruusan|final Battle of Ruusan]], the once-idealistic youth had become a cynical and haggard veteran. His closest friend and main [[Advisor/Legends|advisor]] was fellow veteran Pernicar.<ref name="Jedi vs Sith 3" />

Hoth was a very able military commander, having led Republic forces to a number of victories over the Sith.<ref name="pod" /> The ragtag Army of Light which he hastily raised was able to drive the Sith away from many planets, despite being ill-equipped and including children among its ranks.<ref name="Jedi vs Sith 1" /><ref name="jk" /> After unifying the Republic's dispersed forces, he believed that concentrating assaults on the Sith Lords themselves, rather than their armies, would draw the armies away from the Core.<ref name="pod" />

Though a leader of a mighty army, Hoth was a man devoted to peace. He was said to be a wily and stalwart general and a master of strategy, and was seen as a hero by the Republic. In the latter stages of the New Sith Wars, his exploits were widely known, even in isolated colonies in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. However, as the war dragged on, Hoth became more and more consumed by his anger, and his inability to control his emotions nearly doomed the entire Jedi campaign.<ref name="pod" />

Hoth clashed continually with his contemporary Lord Farfalla, whose foppish nature was the direct opposite of Hoth's own pragmatism. Unlike the charismatic Farfalla, Hoth had little time for diplomacy, and his blunt nature often brought him into conflict with his peers. Moreover, in the last days of his life, he had come to hate the Sith, though he was able to resist the temptation to fall.<ref name="pod" /> Hoth only sought peace with Farfalla after being advised to do so by the recently killed Pernicar, who appeared to him in a dream. Pernicar also advised Hoth to trust in the Force; the words of wisdom solved a crisis of faith that Hoth was undergoing, in which he had seriously considered abandoning Ruusan to the Sith. Although he had not welcomed the idea, the increasingly desperate situation and the poor morale of his troops had weighed heavily on him. Ultimately, Hoth's decision to combine his forces with Farfalla turned the tide of the Ruusan campaign to their favor.<ref name="Jedi vs Sith 6" />

When he realized that he had to go into certain doom to ensure the fall of the Brotherhood of Darkness through either their mass surrender, destruction in battle with the Jedi, or unleashing the thought bomb, he ordered the other Jedi Masters to pull back with most of their forces, going in with a heavy heart himself with ninety-nine other fully-informed Jedi volunteers to die, sacrificing themselves to force the use of the bomb and the destruction of the Brotherhood of Darkness.<ref name="pod" />

==Powers and abilities==
During the Seventh Battle of Russan, Lord Hoth killed thousands of the Sith's warriors and could reap his foes down with his blade, though he was not fast enough to kill Lord Kaan ere the Sith used his Thought Bomb. He also could conjure [[Force Stasis/Legends|stasis fields]] to cage Sith, even without looking at them. Moreover, he had a [[Force vision|premonition]] when all the Dark Lords, guided by Darth Bane, were using a [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith ritual]] to rain hundreds lightning bolts from the sky on his army, so he was able to give the order to flee just in time. Hoth could [[Force sense/Legends|sense]] when something sinister was afoot and by the Force he knew when he fought the Sith's petty minions and there were no Dark Lords in the detachment he faced. He also could use [[Force empathy/Legends|Force empathy]] to get an idea of the emotions of those around him, such as the joy of the army when it looked like the Brotherhood of Darkness had surrendered. Hoth was learned in the lore of Sith Magic enough to know what a Thought Bomb was and [[Sense Force|sensed]] the massive power of the bomb when he drew close to it.<ref name="pod" /> Before that, he won all but two of the battles of Russan and while his men and women suffered greatly, under his command ninety percent of the Brotherhood of Darkness's soldiers there died,<ref name="NEC" /> and delivered the Corellian Trade Spine from Sith pirates.<ref name="EGTW" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|I suspect the name was originally chosen to be symbolic of his demeanor: cold, grim and harsh.|Drew Karpyshyn|<ref name="Drew's statement" />}}
[[File:HothSketch.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Hoth character study for his appearance in ''Jedi vs. Sith'']]
Lord Hoth was created by [[William C. Dietz]] for the [[1998]] graphic novella ''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]'', in which he appears in a vision to Kyle Katarn.<ref name="jk" /> The character also features prominently in [[Darko Macan]]'s [[2001]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic series]] ''[[Star Wars: Jedi vs. Sith]]''<ref name="Jedi vs Sith 1" /> and in [[Drew Karpyshyn]]'s [[2006]] novel ''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]''.<ref name="pod" /> The [[Water/Legends|icy]] planet of [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]], famous for appearing in the [[1980]] film {{Film|V}}, shares a name with Lord Hoth,<ref name="esb">{{Film|V}}</ref> and although Valenthyne Farfalla remarks in ''Jedi vs. Sith'' that "Lord Hoth's heart is as cold as an ice planet,"<ref name="Jedi vs Sith 5">[[Jedi vs. Sith 5|''Jedi vs. Sith'' 5]]</ref> Karpyshyn stated that, to his knowledge, there was no [[in-universe]] connection between the two. He further speculated that Macan intended the name to represent the character's cold, grim demeanor.<ref name="Drew's statement">{{TORweb|url=community/showthread.php?p=2993503#edit2993503|text=***?!? Is Hoth really called Hoth yet?}}</ref>

However, [[2012]]'s ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' finally established a connection between man and planet. Authors [[Jason Fry]] and [[Paul R. Urquhart]] were hesitant due to the world's isolation in ''The Empire Strikes Back''<ref name="endnotes">{{Tumblr|subdomain=jasonfry|url=post/21728447315/eg-to-warfare-endnotes-pt-4|name=Jason Fry's Dorkery|text=EG to Warfare: Endnotes Pt. 4}}</ref>—set in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>—but they ultimately decided to do so when the [[2011]] [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|MMORPG]] ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' established that it was a well-known planet thousands of years before the events of ''Jedi vs. Sith''. Urquhart has stated that the decision paid quiet homage to a fantasy he had as a child of ancient Jedi Knights riding [[Tauntaun/Legends|tauntauns]] on the icy world. Urquhart also speculated that Hoth may have been named after the famed [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Rohlan Dyre]], who, at the times of the New Sith Wars, could have been viewed as a hero by Jedi troubadours.<ref name="endnotes" /> Urquart has also revealed that his original proposal for Hoth's birth name was Kyprianus von Urron zu Hoth and that the name of the character [[Makkor von Urron]] is intended to refer to this name.<ref name="rise of the empire">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-part-10-the-rise-of-the-empire|series=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|text=Part 10: The Rise of the Empire}}</ref>

There is a discrepancy in Hoth's description between ''Dark Forces: Jedi Knight'' and later sources. ''Jedi Knight'' describes him at the time of the Battle of Ruusan as "a giant of a man with shoulder-length blond hair, a lantern-shaped jaw, and ice-blue eyes," while ''Jedi vs. Sith'' and all sources based on it show him to have gray hair and brown eyes.

[[File:Jedi_Temple_stained_glass_concept_art.png|thumb|right|250px|Jedi Temple stained glass concept art in ''Star Wars: The Clone Wars The Complete Season Two'']]
There are also conflicting accounts of the battles of Ruusan between ''Jedi vs. Sith'' and ''Path of Destruction''. The most significant of these differences is the timing of Pernicar's death with relation to Farfalla's arrival and his reconciliation with Hoth. ''Jedi vs. Sith'' has Pernicar killed in the [[Sixth Battle of Ruusan]], when the Dark Lords of the Sith use the Force to unleash a firestorm across the surface of Ruusan. In ''Darth Bane: Path of Destruction'', some time before the sixth battle, Pernicar and Hoth are traveling in a supply convoy in the forests of Ruusan when they are ambushed by the Sith. Pernicar is killed in the attack, but Hoth is saved by the timely arrival of Valenthyne Farfalla. Both stories share several common elements—these include Pernicar dying in the vicinity of Hoth, Farfalla arriving shortly after, and Hoth being angry at Farfalla over Pernicar's death before later apologizing. There has been no official explanation or harmonization of these two accounts.

Hoth appeared in the artwork by [[Wayne Lo]] titled "Unused Jedi Temple mosaic" for the [[The Clone Wars: Season Two|second season]] of the ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' television series. It was included in the production journal of the ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars The Complete Season Two]]''. The concept art also depicted Valenthyne Farfalla leading the Army of Light and the death of Pernicar.<ref name="TCW CS2">[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars The Complete Season Two|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' The Complete Season Two]]</ref>
